problemGamblingPrevalence.note: Problem gambling prevalence among 11-16 year olds in England

onlineGambling1624.note: Percentage of 16-24 year olds who gambled online in the past month

childrenTreated.note: Children treated for gambling harm through NHS and charity services

sources.name: Gambling Commission
sources.dataset: Gambling Participation and Problem Gambling Statistics

sources.name: NHS
sources.dataset: National Problem Gambling Clinic — Treatment Statistics

metadata.methodology: Youth problem gambling prevalence from Gambling Commission annual Young People and Gambling Survey, using DSM-IV Problem Gambling Scale. Online gambling rates from GC Participation Survey (adults) and Young People Survey. Treatment figures from NHS National Problem Gambling Clinic and GamCare treatment statistics.
knownIssues: Loot box spending in video games may not be captured as gambling in all surveys
Under-18 treatment figures likely underestimate true treatment need due to limited service capacity
Online gambling figures include sports betting, casino games, lottery — not uniform across surveys
